messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: hey @skykingisepic , there are a few discord servers for TN: TN Mesh, Nash Mesh, and The Comms Channel. Let me know if you need an invite to them. They are adding actively building out meshcore mesh in TN.
messages.content_html: hey @skykingisepic , there are a few discord servers for TN: TN Mesh, Nash Mesh, and The Comms Channel. Let me know if you need an invite to them. They are adding actively building out meshcore mesh in TN.

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: Just blew out of TNMesh and NashMesh- too much useless noise and I doubt they are open to Core people invasion...CommsChannel folks are switched on and see the futurea and are switching 'tastic to Core so I'll stay in their group. (edited)
messages.content_html: Just blew out of TNMesh and NashMesh- too much useless noise and I doubt they are open to Core people invasion...CommsChannel folks are switched on and see the futurea and are switching 'tastic to Core so I'll stay in their group. (edited)

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: some people on TNMesh and Nash Mesh are adding meshcore repeaters next to their meshtastic repeaters. It will take time. Once they have a taste of meshcore, they will move to meshcore. but you are correct, I think you will have a much better time working @TheCommsChannel building out meshcore.
messages.content_html: some people on TNMesh and Nash Mesh are adding meshcore repeaters next to their meshtastic repeaters. It will take time. messages.author: TheCommsChannel
messages.text: Meshcore is the way forward. Being amoung the first in TN to deploy Meshtastic, many of us have no desire to continue to deploy it in an wide geographical area. Seeing what these other Meshes are doing with Meshcore was definitely eye opening.
messages.content_html: Meshcore is the way forward. Being amoung the first in TN to deploy Meshtastic, many of us have no desire to continue to deploy it in an wide geographical area. Seeing what these other Meshes are doing with Meshcore was definitely eye opening.
messages.reactions_html:
2
1

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: New H4 is here and the Packet Capture FW is flashed and reporting via WiFi direct (no external device running app required). These are going to become very popular. Will be replacing my RAK up on solar pole with this soon.
messages.content_html: New H4 is here and the Packet Capture FW is flashed and reporting via WiFi direct (no external device running app required). These are going to become very popular. Will be replacing my RAK up on solar pole with this soon.

messages.author: TidusFox9
messages.text: Is the TheCommsChannel group intended to extend as far as Memphis? I know that's a lot of geography to cover, but I also haven't found a Memphis/Midsouth MeshCore group (edited)
messages.content_html: Is the TheCommsChannel group intended to extend as far as Memphis? I know that's a lot of geography to cover, but I also haven't found a Memphis/Midsouth MeshCore group (edited)

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: they are extending toward west, hopefully to Nashville in the near future and then further to Memphis. I just put up a repeater in Nashville near Music Row. did some wardriving in the area as well. Start your mesh in Memphis now and then it will be ready to join up to Nashville/Knoxville/Chattanooga when the gaps are filled in. https://discord.gg/qFYehnvQsm

messages.author: Tackleberry
messages.text: @TidusFox9 I am just west of Nashville in Dickson County near I-40/I-840, and i have relatives in Jackson and Memphis but I work in Nashville. My hope is to help set some up along I-40 where I am to get it headed that direction - Memphis. Would love to have statewide off grid comms... (edited)

messages.author: TidusFox9
messages.text: I also have family a few cities south of Nashville, so having a few repeaters along the I-40 to bridge the gap would be great. Unfortunately, I live in the southwest corner of the city and I don't have access to set up a repeater high enough to cover any significant territory. I'll join TCC and I should be setting up a home repeater this month. Next month, I'll probably be setting up another repeater and some client devices for local family if I can get the buy-in.

messages.author: TidusFox9
messages.text: The map at meshcore.co.uk/map.html says the two repeaters in Nashville are using CR 8 and SF 7, while most of the other repeaters I see around the state are the default CR 5 SF 7. Are these CR/SF values set for these repeaters intentionally? Would that make them invisible to devices using the default CR/SF?
messages.content_html: The map at meshcore.co.uk/map.html says the two repeaters in Nashville are using CR 8 and SF 7, while most of the other repeaters I see around the state are the default CR 5 SF 7. Are these CR/SF values set for these repeaters intentionally? Would that make them invisible to devices using the default CR/SF?
messages.reply_html:
Uncle Lit
they are extending toward west, hopefully to Nashville in the near future and then further to Memphis. I just put up a repeater in Nashville near Music Row. did some wardriving in the area as well. Start your mesh in Memphis now and then it will be ready to join up to Nashville/Knoxville/Chattanooga when the gaps are filled in. https://discord.gg/qFYehnvQsm

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: CR 5, 6, 7, 8 are all compatible with each other given the same SF, BW, and frequency. In Tennessee, choose USA/Canada (Recommended) preset. You can keep CR at 5, or change to 8 if connection is weak.
messages.content_html: CR 5, 6, 7, 8 are all compatible with each other given the same SF, BW, and frequency. In Tennessee, choose USA/Canada (Recommended) preset. You can keep CR at 5, or change to 8 if connection is weak.

messages.text: Ah, okay, I didn't realize CR could be so variable. Thanks!

messages.reply_html:
Uncle Lit
CR 5, 6, 7, 8 are all compatible with each other given the same SF, BW, and frequency. In Tennessee, choose USA/Canada (Recommended) preset. You can keep CR at 5, or change to 8 if connection is weak.
